validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_hook_python_detection -v passes 5/5 (0.002s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 227/228 with 1 OS-specific skip (37.3s, up from 223 -- 5 new cases for unit_bugfix_001)

checkpoint.summary: unit_bugfix_001 complete. ISSUE_001 RESOLVED. skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 5 'Hook configuration' subsection now leads with the portable Python-detection idiom (PY=$(command -v python3 2>/dev/null || command -v python 2>/dev/null) with the [ -z "$PY" ] exit-1 guard -- the same pattern used by all 7 workspace command templates + SKILL.md) and bakes the resolved interpreter into the generated command field for BOTH tool variants ('"$PY .claude/hooks/continue-loop.py"' for Claude Code, '"$PY .cursor/hooks/continue-loop.py"' for Cursor). The doc explicitly instructs writing the resolved absolute path into the JSON -- the literal '$PY' must not end up in the generated file. Hook script's '#!/usr/bin/env python3' shebang preserved for direct-invocation ergonomics but is never resolved at hook-execution time, fixing the Windows exit-127 trap. New test_hook_python_detection.py (120 LOC, 5 cases) pins the contract at the doc level: detection idiom present, exact $PY-command-field strings for both variants, forbidden-substring check against the pre-fix bare-path template, and the 'do not leave literal $PY' guidance assertion. Section-slicing helper guards against unrelated Python-detection snippets elsewhere in the doc accidentally satisfying the assertions. ISSUE_001.json flipped to 'resolved' with timestamp 2026-04-20T09:30:00Z. state.json issues counter updated: total 2, open 1, resolved 1. Suite 223 -> 228.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_bugfix_002 (PHASE_011): ISSUE_002 fix -- retire the Stop-hook-as-continuation-driver for Claude Code (Cursor hook stays unchanged). (a) Add new doc skills/development-harness/references/claude-code-continuation.md explaining why Cursor's followup_message protocol cannot port to Claude Code's one-shot stop_hook_active guard. (b) Rewrite skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py to a precondition-only shape: run the authority chain (flag, budget, blockers, open_questions, selector-vs-checkpoint agreement), print an advisory to stdout describing next_unit or the reason to stop, always exit 0; do not attempt exit-2 block-continue. (c) Add 'Claude Code: run under /loop' sections to skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md and skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md pointing Claude Code users at '/loop /invoke-development-harness' as the autonomous-run primitive. (d) Update skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 5 so Claude Code installs wire the hook as precondition-checker (not continue-driver). Large unit -- probably split across multiple commits within this unit.
